[REDACTED]

Vite loads the config, which pulls `@workspace/api-client/org-next` via the plugin chain. Node's ESM resolver won't resolve extensionless `./campaignContacts` to `.ts`. Checking relative imports across the org-next hooks package:

[REDACTED]

Adding `.js` extensions to relative imports so Node's ESM loader can resolve them when Vite loads the config and pulls `@workspace/api-client/org-next` (TypeScript source) through the plugin chain.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Reverting `.js` imports: Node resolves `campaignContacts.js` to a file that doesn't exist (source is `.ts`). The failure chain is the Vite config importing code that pulls in `@workspace/api-client`. Checking the stub and breaking that chain.

[REDACTED]

Reverting the `.js` edits. Node resolves `*.js` to real files, so `campaignContacts.js` doesn't exist alongside `campaignContacts.ts`. The Vite config loads `fake-org-api-data.ts`, which imports the `org-next` barrel and pulls the entire graph through Node's native resolver. Pointing those imports at `types.ts` only avoids loading the barrel. Reverting the `.js` changes and adding a `org-next/types` export.

[REDACTED]

input.description: Revert .js suffixes on relative imports in org-next hooks
Verify Vite config loads and build starts

content.text:
Here is what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause

`vite.config.ts` loads `vite-plugin-local-org-api.ts`, which imports `local-org-api-stub.ts`, which imports `fake-org-api-data.ts`. That file imported from `@workspace/api-client/org-next`, which resolves to `index.ts` and **re-exports every hook**. While Vite is loading the config, Node’s ESM loader walks that graph and tries to resolve **extensionless** paths like `./campaignContacts`. Node does not apply TypeScript resolution there, so you get `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`.

### Fix

1. **`packages/api-client/package.json`** — Added a dedicated export so consumers can depend on types only:

   `"./org-next/types": "./src/hooks/org-next/types.ts"`

2. **`apps/org-next/src/server/fake-org-api-data.ts`** and **`local-org-api-stub.ts`** — Switched imports from `@workspace/api-client/org-next` to `@workspace/api-client/org-next/types`, which only loads `types.ts` (and `zod`), not the full hooks barrel.

`vite build` and `turbo typecheck` for those packages succeed after this.

**Note:** For anything that only needs shared types or `makeEmailAddress`, prefer `@workspace/api-client/org-next/types`. Keep `@workspace/api-client/org-next` for hooks and runtime API code loaded through Vite’s normal bundling path, not from the Vite config graph.

[REDACTED]
